title: Smoky Creamy German Poppy Seeds Curry
description: A nutty, creamy German-inspired curry featuring ground poppy seeds and a smoky tandoor twist, perfect for a halal-friendly crowd-pleaser.
instructions: Prepare a tandoor or preheat your oven to 200°C (392°F).
Wash and rinse the rice until the water runs clear; set aside.
Dice the red onion and mince the garlic cloves.
In a small bowl, combine the ground poppy seeds, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, and garam masala; set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the spice mixture and cook for another minute, letting the spices toast slightly.
Pour in the coconut milk and low-sodium chicken broth, then stir in the tomato paste. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the chicken breast pieces to the skillet, cover, and let the curry cook for 15 minutes, turning the chicken halfway through.

While the chicken simmers, place the remaining 1 tbsp of oil in a small oven-safe dish and lightly brush the top with oil. Place the dish in the preheated oven to heat and become slightly smoky—the tandoor effect.
Once the chicken is cooked (internal temperature 75°C/165°F), remove from heat and stir in the preheated tandoor-oiled dish to infuse a subtle smoky flavor. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a separate pot, bring water to a boil, add the rinsed rice, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes or until the rice is fluffy.
Serve the smoky poppy seed curry over the cooked basmati rice, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
Enjoy your creamy, smoky German poppy seeds curry—a halal-friendly crowd-pleaser!
